title: Arthur Sellers (Scottish cricketer)

infobox.value: (23 February 1876 — 9 February 1949) was an [English](/wiki/English_people "English people")-born [Scottish](/wiki/Scottish_people "Scottish people") first-class [cricketer](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") and groundskeeper.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish cricketer

Not to be confused with the English cricketer [Arthur Sellers](/wiki/Arthur_Sellers "Arthur Sellers").

**Arthur Ernest Sellers** (23 February 1876 — 9 February 1949) was an [English](/wiki/English_people "English people")-born [Scottish](/wiki/Scottish_people "Scottish people") first-class [cricketer](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") and groundskeeper.

Sellers was born at [Mansfield](/wiki/Mansfield "Mansfield") in February 1876. He moved to Scotland as a young child when his father, Tom Sellers, moved there to take up a groundskeeping job at [The Grange Club](/wiki/The_Grange_Club "The Grange Club").[[1]](#cite_note-1) A [club cricketer](/wiki/Club_cricket "Club cricket") for Carlton Cricket Club, he played two [first-class matches](/wiki/First-class_cricket "First-class cricket") for [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team") in 1920 and 1922; he was a late debutant in first-class cricket, being aged 44 in 1920. His appearance in 1920 came against [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland_cricket_team "Ireland cricket team") at [Edinburgh](/wiki/The_Grange_Club "The Grange Club"), with his 1922 appearance coming against the [Marylebone Cricket Club](/wiki/Marylebone_Cricket_Club "Marylebone Cricket Club") at [Lord's](/wiki/Lord%27s "Lord's").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Playing as a right-arm [medium pace bowler](/wiki/Seam_bowling "Seam bowling") in the Scottish side, Sellers took 8 wickets at an [average](/wiki/Bowling_average "Bowling average") of 19.12;.[[3]](#cite_note-3) he took one [five wicket haul](/wiki/Five_wicket_haul "Five wicket haul"), with figures of 5 for 22 against Ireland.[[4]](#cite_note-4) A knee injury later ended his club career while he was still engaged as a professional at Carlton.[[5]](#cite_note-NEWS-5)

Outside of cricket, Sellers was a groundsman. He was employed by [Scottish Rugby Union](/wiki/Scottish_Rugby_Union "Scottish Rugby Union") and was responsible for preparing all of Scotland's home international rugby pitches at [Inverleith](/wiki/Inverleith_Sports_Ground "Inverleith Sports Ground") and [Murrayfield](/wiki/Murrayfield_Stadium "Murrayfield Stadium").[[5]](#cite_note-NEWS-5) Sellers died at [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh") in February 1949.
